one_line: Shivaji Maharaj’s loyal companion who risked his life by impersonating him during the escape from Agra Fort in 1666.

appearance.age_look: Mid-20s, lean but wiry build with the toned physique of a trained swordsman used to both courtly presence and sudden action.
appearance.face: Oval face with a straight nose, medium-full lips, neatly kept short moustache and small pointed beard, wheatish-brown Deccan complexion, a faint scar on his left cheek, and a calm, alert default expression that hints at quiet bravery.
appearance.hair: Thick, dark brown-black hair gathered into a low, neat knot at the back of his head, mostly covered by a soft saffron pagri in Mughal-court style, with a narrow white cloth band running around its base and the knot subtly visible at the back.
appearance.costume: He wears a finely tailored long angarkha styled like a Mughal court robe but in Maratha taste, knee-length and crossing right-over-left, made of lightweight off-white cotton with a subtle vertical weave; over this is a sleeveless quilted cotton waistcoat (padded but not bulky) in muted bottle-green with simple stitched diamond patterns; a narrow red-and-gold woven sash (kamarband) wraps snugly around his waist and knots slightly to the left, with the loose end hanging; his churidar-style trousers are ankle-length and plain cream; on his feet are brown leather mojari with slightly upturned toes and simple stitching; he also wears a single small gold hoop earring in his left ear and a plain iron bangle on his right wrist to suggest a personal, non-flashy piety.
appearance.signature: A sheathed, slightly curved talwar with a simple steel hilt and no elaborate inlay, hanging from his red-and-gold sash on his left side, the scabbard clad in dark brown leather with a plain brass chape so it looks practical rather than ceremonial.

ref_desc: Hiroji Farzand is a lean mid-20s Maratha with wheatish complexion, short moustache and small beard, saffron pagri over a low hair-knot, off-white angarkha, bottle-green quilted sleeveless waistcoat, cream churidar, brown mojari and a plain dark-brown-sheathed talwar on a red-and-gold sash.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character sheet of Hiroji Farzand as a dignified Maratha companion in 1666 Agra Fort: a lean mid-20s man with wheatish-brown skin, an oval face, straight nose, short neat moustache, small pointed beard and a faint scar on his left cheek, wearing a calm, alert expression; his thick dark brown-black hair is tied in a low knot at the back, mostly covered by a soft saffron pagri in Mughal court style with a narrow white band around its base; he wears a knee-length off-white cotton angarkha crossing right over left, over which sits a sleeveless bottle-green quilted cotton waistcoat with stitched diamond patterns, cinched by a narrow red-and-gold woven sash tied slightly to the left, cream churidar trousers fitting closely at the calves, and brown leather mojari with slightly upturned toes; add a single small gold hoop earring in his left ear and a plain iron bangle on his right wrist; his signature weapon, a sheathed slightly curved talwar with a simple steel hilt and dark brown leather scabbard with a plain brass tip, hangs from the sash on his left side; show front, side and back views with consistent colours and details in a clean shonen-anime style while keeping the outfit historically grounded to late 17th-century Maratha presence in Mughal Agra.
